task_completion.description: Agent opened the furniture design brief on the Desktop to read the schedule.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or viewer open showing the contents of 'furniture_brief.txt' from the Desktop.

task_completion.description: Agent loaded the FZK-Haus IFC model in Blender/Bonsai.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Blender interface open with the FZK-Haus architectural model visible in the 3D viewport or the outliner showing IFC building elements.

task_completion.description: Agent created at least 3 distinct IfcFurnitureType definitions.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Blender outliner (in IFC Project view) or Bonsai IFC properties panel showing the creation/existence of multiple 'IfcFurnitureType' entities (e.g., Sofa, Dining Table).

task_completion.description: Agent created at least 6 IfcFurniture instances in the model.
task_completion.visual_evidence: 3D viewport showing new furniture objects added to the house, or the outliner listing at least 6 'IfcFurniture' entities.

task_completion.description: Agent applied a material to at least one furniture element using IFC material associations.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Bonsai Object Properties > IFC Material panel showing an IFC material assigned to a selected furniture object.

task_completion.description: Agent ensured the furniture is spatially contained within a building storey.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Outliner showing furniture objects nested under an 'IfcBuildingStorey' node, or the Bonsai spatial containment properties panel showing the assignment to a storey.

task_completion.description: Agent saved the furnished IFC model to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Blender save/export dialog showing the path '/home/ga/BIMProjects/fzk_furnished.ifc', or a terminal/file manager confirming the file was created.

integrity.description: Agent used the Blender/Bonsai GUI to model and assign properties.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with Blender menus, 3D viewport, and Bonsai IFC panels, rather than just running a Python script in the terminal to generate the file.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the IFC data structure via the Bonsai add-on.
integrity.visual_evidence: Bonsai/BlenderBIM specific panels (e.g., Object Properties > IFC Object, Scene Properties > IFC Project) are visible and actively used to set types, materials, and containment.

integrity.description: Agent did not simply create a text file named fzk_furnished.ifc and paste raw IFC text into it.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file is saved via Blender's 'Save Project' or IFC export functionality, not written manually in a generic text editor.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The design brief is located at '/home/ga/Desktop/furniture_brief.txt'. The agent must create at least 6 IfcFurniture instances and at least 3 distinct IfcFurnitureType definitions. The final output must be saved exactly to '/home/ga/BIMProjects/fzk_furnished.ifc'. The task requires using Blender with the Bonsai (BlenderBIM) add-on to author the IFC file, including material assignments and spatial containment within an IfcBuildingStorey.
